fault_hypothesis: The overlap detection on line 65 uses non-strict inequalities (`<=`, `>=`), which incorrectly fails to detect conflicts when intervals are adjacent (touching at boundaries). The non-overlap condition should use strict inequalities (`<`, `>`).

constraints.intent: If the code proceeds past the conflict check, the intervals must be strictly non-overlapping.
At the end of each check, if no conflict was found, the new interval must be strictly before or after the existing one.

spec.expr: all(interval[1] < s or interval[0] > e for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: If the function is about to accept the interval, it must not have had any boundary conflicts with any of the original slots.
